solution.p: We will factor y^{2}+y-42 where a=1, b=1, and c=-42.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.
For y^{2}+y-42, since the product of -6 and 7 is -42, and the sum of -6 and 7 is 1, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+y-42\amp =\left(y-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(y+7\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (-42) = -42.

The number -42 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-42)\amp -1(42)\amp 2(-21)\amp -2(21)
ewline3(-14)\amp -3(14)\amp 6(-7)\amp -6(7)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 1, and this sum happens with 7+(-6)=1 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+y-42\amp =y^{2}+7y-6y-42
ewline \amp =y\mathopen{}\left(y+7ight)-6\mathopen{}\left(y+7ight)
ewline \amp =\left(y-6ight)\mathopen{}\left(y+7ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in y^{2}+y-42 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}

The third term, -42, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-42)\amp -1(42)\amp 2(-21)\amp -2(21)\newline3(-14)\amp -3(14)\amp 6(-7)\amp -6(7)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with y^{2}+y-42 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+y-42\amp =\left(y-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(y+7\right)\end{aligned}
